noSubtitles: Back to your local model peasant
But that's okay, because everything is running smoothly..right?

lastDecision.rationale: The post explicitly says Codex usage limits were reset already, with another full reset coming within the hour.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The post explicitly says Codex usage limits were reset already, with another full reset coming within the hour.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Codex usage limits will be fully reset again in the next hour and we will credit one additional reset into your bank for your own usage over the next 24 hours.

We investigated reports that Codex usage was being consumed faster than expected. There wasn't one central issue, but a few smaller problems compounded for some users.   

Here's what we found and changed: 
- Actual usage: Auto-review had become more proactive, another change was triggering more subagent work, and background suggestions could run twice or retry too frequently after failures. We reverted the changes and fixed suggestion scheduling, duplicate generation, and retry behavior. This should reduce unnecessary background token consumption while preserving the work users explicitly request. 
- Usage reporting: Auto-review was incorrectly appearing as GPT‑5.4 usage, and failed or rate-limited requests were still shown as turns. Auto-review now appears as its own category, and only successful requests count toward the turn graphs. Rate-limited requests were never charged, but they were being displayed incorrectly. 
- Immediate relief: We reset usage limits while rolling out the fixes, then shipped hotfixes across the CLI, desktop app, and usage backend. 
- What to expect: New usage data should be clearer and actual consumption should be lower. Historical charts may still show auto-review under GPT‑5.4 because older turn data was not relabeled. Features that intentionally perform more work; such as /goal, subagents, and higher reasoning levels will still naturally use more capacity.  

All fixes are now deployed, and we've added more detailed monitoring so we can detect background-usage regressions sooner. We'll continue watching the results closely.

Thank you for building and doing all sorts of things with Codex.

recentEvaluations.rationale: This is a product update about Codex permissions and says nothing about usage limits resetting.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Advanced Codex users. We shipped a replacement to coarse sandbox modes: reusable, inheritable permission profiles binding OS-enforced file read/write/deny rules (even **/*.env) to per-domain network + Unix sockets. Plus fail-closed admin allowlists. Least privilege per task.

https://t.co/jHyAnUhyFs

recentEvaluations.rationale: This is just a thank-you reply and gives no information about limits or resets.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: @nicdunz Thx!

recentEvaluations.rationale: This reply only says to wait and does not explicitly state that any ChatGPT or Codex limit has reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: @ShashankDe5535 You'll have to wait a bit to enjoy that

recentEvaluations.rationale: It discusses past and future manual resets during an investigation, not a current reset being available now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: If you happened to have used your previous reset in the few hours before and didn't go through your usage, do not worry, you will get more manual resets after we conclude the investigation.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet explicitly says everyone's Codex usage limits have been hard reset and are available again now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
As we are still investigating, I have reset everyone's Codex usage limits. This is a hard reset given some users had stacked up to three banked resets already that they can apply on their own schedule.

Funnily enough, this week at OpenAI is called the RESET week, which is meant for folks to relax a bit. However it will be a different kind of RESET week. Enjoy.

recentEvaluations.rationale: This reply discusses model routing for auto review and says nothing about limits or resets.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: @solipsnitsyn Auto review is using GPT-5.4. We do not route. Do not spread misinformation, thank you.

recentEvaluations.rationale: This reply is about YouTube recommendations and does not mention any ChatGPT or Codex limit reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: @Dimillian Not curating your Youtube enough I see

recentEvaluations.rationale: This discusses investigating abnormal usage drains, not that any Codex or ChatGPT limits have reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: Codex team is in a warroom on a Sunday combing through logs and checking whether there is anything that could lead to increased usage drains for some users. Taking it very seriously and won't rest until we get to the bottom of it.

recentEvaluations.rationale: This reply asks about using auto-review and does not mention limits, caps, or resets.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: @SAlbahra Do you use "auto-review"?

recentEvaluations.rationale: The post explicitly says downgrading plans will not reset usage limits.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Going from pro to plus gets you less usage, so if you already consumed your pro limits, downgrading to plus will not reset things for you. 

It's like buying a large coffee at starbucks, drinking it and then throwing the cup away and asking for a smaller cup. You wouldn't expect coffee to be in the small cup I think?

recentEvaluations.rationale: It discusses what consumes weekly usage in the background, not a limit reset or restored availability.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Three things that can consume usage in the background  

- automations 
- memory 
- suggested prompts  

and for memory and suggested prompts they use a small percentage, around 1% of the weekly limit each under normal conditions. You can disable all three in the settings if you wish.
